description: A fully self-hosted, zero-trust Kubernetes platform. Clone, configure, run. Produces a complete production-grade development environment with GitOps, observability, testing, and AI-native project management.

stories.title: Scaffold repo structure and global Helm values
stories.description: Create the complete directory structure outlined in CLAUDE.md. Create a global values file at charts/_globals/values.yaml with domain, storageClass, and keycloak placeholders. Create a config.yaml.example that users fill in before running bootstrap. Create a minimal README.md explaining the project and prerequisites.
acceptanceCriteria: All directories from CLAUDE.md repo structure exist
charts/_globals/values.yaml exists with global.domain, global.storageClass, global.keycloak stubs
config.yaml.example exists with comments explaining every field
README.md explains what Sovereign is, prerequisites, and links to docs/quickstart.md
shellcheck passes on any .sh files created

stories.title: Bootstrap script: generic VPS and Hetzner provisioning
stories.description: Create bootstrap/bootstrap.sh as the main entry point that reads config.yaml and calls the appropriate provider script. Create bootstrap/providers/hetzner.sh using the Hetzner Cloud CLI (hcloud) to provision a CX21 or larger server, install K3s, and output kubeconfig. Create bootstrap/providers/generic-vps.sh that SSH-installs K3s on any Ubuntu 22.04 server given an IP and SSH key. Both must print estimated cost and Cloudflare wildcard DNS instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/bootstrap.sh reads config.yaml and routes to correct provider
bootstrap/providers/hetzner.sh provisions server via hcloud CLI and outputs kubeconfig
bootstrap/providers/generic-vps.sh SSH-installs K3s on any Ubuntu 22.04 server
Both scripts print monthly cost estimate and Cloudflare DNS setup instructions
bootstrap/verify.sh checks that kubectl can connect and nodes are Ready

stories.title: Bootstrap scripts: AWS EC2 free tier and DigitalOcean
stories.description: Create bootstrap/providers/aws-ec2.sh using the AWS CLI to provision a t2.micro (free tier) or t3.small, install K3s, output kubeconfig and cost estimate. Create bootstrap/providers/digitalocean.sh using doctl to provision the cheapest Droplet ($4-6/mo), install K3s, output kubeconfig. Create docs/providers/hetzner.md, docs/providers/aws-ec2-free-tier.md, docs/providers/digitalocean.md with step-by-step human instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrap/providers/aws-ec2.sh provisions EC2 instance and installs K3s
bootstrap/providers/digitalocean.sh provisions Droplet and installs K3s
Both scripts print cost estimates and DNS instructions
All three provider docs exist with prerequisites, steps, and copy-pasteable commands

stories.title: Helm chart: Cilium CNI with network policies
stories.description: Create charts/cilium/ as a Helm chart that wraps the official Cilium chart with opinionated defaults: deny-all default network policy, Hubble UI enabled, WireGuard encryption between nodes. Create argocd-apps/platform/cilium-app.yaml. Cilium must use global.domain for any ingress (Hubble UI).
acceptanceCriteria: Default network policy is deny-all with explicit allow for DNS and same-namespace

stories.title: Helm chart: Crossplane with Helm and Kubernetes providers
stories.description: Create charts/crossplane/ wrapping the official Crossplane chart. Include provider configs for: crossplane-contrib/provider-helm (to install Helm charts via Crossplane), upbound/provider-kubernetes (to manage K8s resources). Create the platform/xrds/ and platform/compositions/ directories with a README explaining the pattern. Create argocd-apps/platform/crossplane-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/crossplane/ chart installs Crossplane and both providers
Provider configs are in templates/ as ProviderConfig resources
platform/xrds/README.md and platform/compositions/README.md explain the XRD pattern

stories.title: Helm charts: cert-manager and Sealed Secrets
stories.description: Create charts/cert-manager/ wrapping jetstack/cert-manager with a self-signed ClusterIssuer for bootstrap and a placeholder for Vault issuer (enabled after Phase 2). Create charts/sealed-secrets/ wrapping bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets. Both get ArgoCD app manifests in argocd-apps/platform/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/cert-manager/ exists with self-signed ClusterIssuer in templates/
Vault issuer template exists but is disabled by default (values flag)

stories.title: ArgoCD root App-of-Apps setup
stories.description: Create charts/argocd/ wrapping argo/argo-cd with opinionated values: SSO stub for Keycloak OIDC (enabled after Phase 2), repo server with the sovereign repo pre-configured, admin password from Sealed Secret. Create argocd-apps/root-app.yaml that watches the argocd-apps/ directory and creates all child apps. Create argocd-apps/platform/, argocd-apps/security/, argocd-apps/observability/, argocd-apps/devex/, argocd-apps/testing/ directories with placeholder README files.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/argocd/ exists with Keycloak OIDC stub in values.yaml (disabled by default)
argocd-apps/root-app.yaml points to the sovereign repo argocd-apps/ directory
All 5 tier subdirectories exist in argocd-apps/ with README.md
Bootstrap script includes step to install ArgoCD and apply root-app.yaml

stories.title: Helm chart: HashiCorp Vault
stories.description: Create charts/vault/ wrapping hashicorp/vault. Dev mode enabled by default for bootstrap (single pod, in-memory). Production HA mode values documented and togglable. Include: auto-unseal config stub, PKI secrets engine initialization script in templates/jobs/, Kubernetes auth method setup. Ingress at vault.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/platform/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/vault/ dev mode works out of box
Production HA values documented in values.yaml with comments
PKI init Job template exists in templates/jobs/
Kubernetes auth method setup Job template exists

stories.title: Helm chart: Keycloak SSO
stories.description: Create charts/keycloak/ wrapping bitnami/keycloak. Bootstrap mode: embedded H2 database. Production mode: external PostgreSQL (Crossplane-managed, togglable). Pre-configure a 'sovereign' realm via init Job that creates: admin user, platform clients (argocd, gitlab, grafana, backstage, sovereign-pm, code-server). Ingress at auth.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/platform/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/keycloak/ works with embedded DB by default
External PostgreSQL values documented and togglable
Init Job creates 'sovereign' realm and all platform OIDC clients
All client redirect URIs use global.domain

stories.title: Helm chart: Rook/Ceph distributed encrypted storage
stories.description: Create charts/rook-ceph/ wrapping rook-release/rook-ceph operator AND rook-release/rook-ceph-cluster as a subchart. CephCluster configured for: encryption at rest (Vault KMS integration stub), 3-replica default, block storage class 'ceph-block', filesystem storage class 'ceph-filesystem', object storage class 'ceph-bucket'. Includes a post-install verification Job. Grafana dashboards for Ceph in a ConfigMap.
acceptanceCriteria: Three StorageClasses created: ceph-block, ceph-filesystem, ceph-bucket
Vault KMS integration documented in values.yaml (disabled by default, togglable)
Encryption at rest enabled by default using cluster-local key if Vault not configured

stories.title: Helm chart: GitLab self-hosted
stories.description: Create charts/gitlab/ wrapping gitlab/gitlab with opinionated values: external object storage pointing to Ceph (MinIO-compatible), PostgreSQL via Crossplane, Redis via Crossplane, Keycloak OIDC auth, GitLab Runner pre-configured. Ingress at gitlab.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. Registry at registry.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. Initial admin credentials from Sealed Secret. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/platform/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/gitlab/ uses Ceph object storage for artifacts/uploads/packages
Keycloak OIDC configured as auth provider
GitLab Runner pre-configured with Kubernetes executor
Ingress uses global.domain for all GitLab subdomains
Initial root password from Sealed Secret

stories.title: Helm chart: Harbor artifact registry
stories.description: Create charts/harbor/ wrapping goharbor/harbor. Storage backend: Ceph S3-compatible (ceph-bucket StorageClass). Keycloak OIDC auth. Trivy scanner integration (internal). Ingress at registry.{{ .Values.global.domain }} (or harbor.{{ .Values.global.domain }} for UI). Replication rules stub for syncing public images. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/platform/.

stories.title: Helm chart: Istio service mesh with mTLS
stories.description: Create charts/istio/ wrapping istio/base and istio/istiod as subcharts. Default PeerAuthentication: STRICT mTLS for all namespaces. Gateway resource for the sovereign ingress domain. Kiali UI at kiali.{{ .Values.global.domain }} with Keycloak OIDC. Jaeger/Tempo integration for tracing. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/security/.

stories.title: Helm charts: OPA/Gatekeeper, Trivy Operator, Falco
stories.description: Create charts/opa-gatekeeper/ wrapping gatekeeper/gatekeeper. Include ConstraintTemplates for: no-privilege-escalation, require-resource-limits, require-labels (app/tier). Create charts/trivy-operator/ wrapping aquasecurity/trivy-operator (scans all running pods). Create charts/falco/ wrapping falcosecurity/falco with JSON output to Loki. All three get ArgoCD apps in argocd-apps/security/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/opa-gatekeeper/ has 3 ConstraintTemplates and corresponding Constraints
charts/falco/ outputs JSON to stdout for Loki scraping
helm lint passes on all three
argocd-apps/security/ has app manifests for all three

stories.title: Helm chart: kube-prometheus-stack
stories.description: Create charts/prometheus-stack/ wrapping prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack. Grafana at grafana.{{ .Values.global.domain }} with Keycloak OIDC. Prometheus retention: 15 days (Thanos handles long-term). AlertManager with a stub for Slack/email routing. Pre-load dashboards: Node Exporter, Kubernetes cluster, GitLab, Ceph (from rook-ceph chart), Istio. Persistent storage on ceph-block.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard ConfigMaps for Node, K8s, GitLab, Ceph, Istio

stories.title: Helm charts: Loki, Thanos, Tempo
stories.description: Create charts/loki/ wrapping grafana/loki in Simple Scalable mode, storage on ceph-bucket. Create charts/thanos/ wrapping bitnami/thanos with sidecar mode (connects to Prometheus), storage on ceph-bucket. Create charts/tempo/ wrapping grafana/tempo-distributed, storage on ceph-bucket. All pre-configured as Grafana datasources (via ConfigMap in prometheus-stack chart). ArgoCD apps in argocd-apps/observability/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/thanos/ connects to Prometheus via sidecar
All three auto-registered as Grafana datasources
helm lint passes on all three
argocd-apps/observability/ has all three app manifests

stories.title: Helm chart: Backstage developer portal
stories.description: Create charts/backstage/ using a custom Dockerfile-based deployment (backstage/backstage base image). Pre-configure plugins: GitLab catalog, Kubernetes plugin, ArgoCD plugin, TechDocs (stored on ceph-bucket). Keycloak OIDC auth. Ingress at backstage.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. Service catalog auto-discovers GitLab repos via catalog-info.yaml. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/devex/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/backstage/ deploys Backstage with Keycloak OIDC
GitLab integration configured (reads catalog-info.yaml from repos)

stories.title: Helm chart: code-server (VS Code in browser for agents)
stories.description: Create charts/code-server/ deploying coder/code-server. This is the primary interface for Claude Code agents operating on the platform. Pre-install extensions: GitLens, YAML, Kubernetes, Helm, Python, ESLint. Mount kubeconfig so agents can run kubectl/helm from browser. Workspace storage on ceph-filesystem. Keycloak OIDC auth. Ingress at code.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/devex/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/code-server/ deploys with Keycloak OIDC auth

stories.title: Helm charts: SonarQube and ReportPortal
stories.description: Create charts/sonarqube/ wrapping sonarqube/sonarqube. PostgreSQL storage via Crossplane. Keycloak OIDC. Ingress at sonar.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. GitLab CI integration documented. Create charts/reportportal/ using reportportal Helm chart. PostgreSQL + Elasticsearch + RabbitMQ dependencies. Keycloak OIDC. Ingress at reports.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. Both in argocd-apps/devex/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/sonarqube/ with Keycloak OIDC and ceph-block storage
charts/reportportal/ with all dependencies and Keycloak OIDC
GitLab CI integration guide in docs/

stories.title: Helm charts: Selenium Grid, k6, Wiremock, MailHog, Chaos Mesh
stories.description: Create 5 testing infrastructure charts: charts/selenium-grid/ (Selenium HQ official chart, Chrome+Firefox+Edge nodes), charts/k6-operator/ (grafana/k6-operator), charts/wiremock/ (wiremock/wiremock), charts/mailhog/ (mailhog deployment), charts/chaos-mesh/ (chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh). All get ArgoCD apps in argocd-apps/testing/. Selenium and MailHog get ingresses. Grafana dashboards for k6 results.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 charts exist with helm lint passing

stories.title: Sovereign PM: Node.js/Express backend
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/src/server/ — a Node.js + Express + TypeScript API server. Data model: Project, Epic, Story, AgentRun. PostgreSQL via Sequelize. Endpoints: CRUD for epics/stories, POST /api/prd/generate (outputs prd.json), POST /api/ralph/trigger (creates a Kubernetes Job running ralph.sh), GET /api/ralph/runs (list run history). Keycloak OIDC JWT validation middleware. OpenAPI spec in sovereign-pm/openapi.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: Sequelize models for Project, Epic, Story, AgentRun
Keycloak JWT middleware on all routes
npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass

stories.title: Sovereign PM: React frontend
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/src/client/ — a React + TypeScript + Vite frontend. Pages: Dashboard (all projects), Project view (epics/stories kanban), Story editor (title, description, acceptance criteria, priority, phase), PRD Generator (shows generated prd.json, copy button), Agent Runs (history of Ralph loops, pass/fail per story, timestamps). Keycloak OIDC via keycloak-js. Tailwind CSS. Clean, minimal design.
acceptanceCriteria: React + TypeScript + Vite app in sovereign-pm/src/client/
PRD Generator shows formatted prd.json with copy button
Agent Runs page shows story pass/fail history
npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass

stories.title: Sovereign PM: Dockerfile and Helm chart
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/Dockerfile (multi-stage: build frontend, build backend, combined production image). Create charts/sovereign-pm/ Helm chart deploying the PM app. PostgreSQL dependency (or external Crossplane-managed). Keycloak OIDC config from values. Ingress at pm.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. Ralph scripts mounted as a volume (reads prd.json from PVC shared with the K8s Job). ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/devex/sovereign-pm-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/sovereign-pm/ deploys app with Keycloak OIDC
Ralph Job template in chart templates/

stories.title: Documentation: quickstart, architecture, and all provider guides
stories.description: Write docs/quickstart.md (15 minute path from zero to running cluster). Write docs/architecture.md (explains the bootstrap sequence, App-of-Apps pattern, namespace layout, security model, all services with their ingress URLs). Write docs/providers/aws-ec2-free-tier.md, docs/providers/digitalocean.md, docs/providers/vultr.md, docs/providers/bare-metal.md. Update README.md with badges, architecture diagram (ASCII), and contribution guide.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/quickstart.md covers: prerequisites, clone repo, fill config.yaml, run bootstrap.sh, verify
docs/architecture.md explains all phases and includes service URL table
All 4 provider docs exist with cost estimates and step-by-step instructions
README.md has ASCII architecture diagram showing service relationships

stories.title: CI pipeline: lint and validate all Helm charts on every PR
stories.description: Create .github/workflows/validate.yml: on PR and push to main, run helm lint on all charts, helm template | kubectl apply --dry-run, shellcheck on all .sh files, markdownlint on all .md files. Create .github/workflows/release.yml: on tag push, package all Helm charts and publish to GitHub Pages as a Helm repository (so users can add sovereign as a Helm repo). Create charts/index.yaml structure for the Helm repo.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/release.yml packages and publishes charts on tag
GitHub Pages Helm repository structure correct
All charts currently pass the lint workflow
README.md includes helm repo add sovereign instructions
